name: Szechuan Shrimp with Chili Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons fresh ginger root, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, devein, and butterfly the shrimp. Sprinkle with 1 teaspoon of salt, cover, and let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Prepare the sauce: mix together hoisin sauce, chili sauce, Shao Hsing, soy sauce, fish sauce, chili paste, sesame oil, peanut oil, hot chili oil, sugar, and MSG.
3. Heat 4 cups of peanut oil in a wok to 375°F (190°C).
4. Wash the shrimp thoroughly with cold water, add another teaspoon of salt, let stand for 30 seconds, then wash off the salt again. Repeat this process twice more. Drain well but do not dry; allow some moisture on the shrimp.
5. Immerse half the shrimp in the hot oil and cook until just past translucent, about 20 seconds. Remove from the oil and set aside.
6. Add the remaining shrimp to the wok, cooking for another 20-30 seconds until finished.
7. Drain all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the wok.
8. Over very high heat, stir-fry ginger, garlic, scallions, and dried chilies for one minute.
9. Add the reserved shrimp and toss to coat with the sauce.
10. Serve immediately.
Note: Chili sauce is an American product available in any supermarket; it was introduced to China early in the 20th century and has become a part of Chinese cuisine. Shao Hsing is rice wine, which can be found at Chinese liquor stores or substituted with dry sherry. Yield: 4 servings
